claims.text: The second wake must consume state produced by the first wake rather than reconstructing it from the full corpus.
Open items are closed by the framework-bound continuation, not by an append-only shortcut.
The model does not choose the terminal surface for the continuation wake.

parsed.conclusion: A multi-wake continuation can be meaningfully compared with append-only output only after it demonstrates that it respects the dependency chain between wakes, closes open items through the framework-bound continuation, and does not usurp the terminal-surface selection that belongs to the framework.
parsed.recommended_action: Given the multi-wake dependency limitation, the comparison protocol should require the multi-wake continuation to (1) prove it consumes state from the first wake without re-deriving it, (2) show that any open items are resolved by the bound continuation rather than a shortcut, and (3) confirm the model never selects the terminal surface itself. Until all three conditions are verified, the multi-wake continuation should not be treated as equivalent to the append-only baseline.
